post.summary: Get access and refresh tokens
post.description:
Use login and password to get a pair of access and refresh tokens.

No authentication header needed. Credentials must be passed in request payload.

post.summary: Refresh access and refresh tokens
post.description:
When access token is expired, call this resource using the refresh token to get a
new pair of tokens.

As opposed to all other resources, this resource must be accessed using the refresh
token, not the access token.

parameters.description: Timezone to use for response data

parameters.name: convert_to
parameters.description: Optional list of units to convert to. If passed, must be of same length as timeseries list. Empty string means no conversion for a timeseries.

get.description:
Returns data in either JSON or CSV format.

JSON: Each key is a timeseries ID as string. For each timeseries, values are
passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries IDs.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

passed as {timestamp: value} mappings.

CSV: The first column is the timestamp and each other column is a timeseries data.
Column headers are timeseries IDs.

In both cases, timestamps are timezone aware datetimes.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
Authentication operations

The following resources are used to get and refresh tokens. When authenticating, first
get a couple of access (short-lived) and refresh (long-lived) tokens using login and
password. When or before access token expires, refresh tokens to get a new pair of
tokens with new expiration dates. If refresh token is expired, get a new pair of tokens
using login and password again.

timezone.description: Timezone used to localize timeseries in this campaign

count.description: Number or values for each bucket

ratio.description: Number or values / expected values for each bucket

avg_ratio.description: Average number or values / expected values

interval.description: Interval between samples (seconds), defined or inferred

undefined_interval.description: Whether the interval was not defined

expected_count.description: Expected number or values for each bucket

timestamps.description: Time index (value is bucket start time)
Time index (value is bucket start time)
